title: Java News Roundup: TornadoVM 5, JHipster, Google ADK, OmniFish Build of Payara, Introducing Vidocq
gist: A weekly digest of Java ecosystem releases: TornadoVM 5.0 adds native GPU execution on NVIDIA hardware, Micronaut patches nearly two dozen Netty CVEs, OmniFish launches paid long-term support for abandoned Payara versions, and a new zero-dependency Jakarta EE/MicroProfile runtime called Vidocq debuts.

insights.claim: TornadoVM 5.0.0 adds the ability to natively run Java on NVIDIA GPUs and a new Hybrid API for calling native NVIDIA libraries directly from a TaskGraph instance, deepening JVM-level integration with CUDA-based GPU compute.
insights.speaker: Michael Redlich
insights.so_what: Signals continued JVM ecosystem investment in direct GPU acceleration, relevant to enterprises running Java-based ML/data workloads that want to avoid rewriting in Python/C++ for GPU access.

themes.theme: Security patching and vendor support economics

insights.claim: Micronaut Framework 5.0.4 upgrades to Netty 4.2.16 to resolve almost two dozen CVEs, reflecting the scale of ongoing dependency-security remediation work in the JVM ecosystem's networking stack.
insights.speaker: Michael Redlich
insights.so_what: A reminder of the recurring patching burden tied to widely-embedded networking libraries like Netty across the Java ecosystem.

insights.claim: OmniFish launched paid, supported builds of legacy Payara Platform 5.0 and 6.0 -- versions no longer supported by Payara itself -- committing to long-term support through 2029 and 2032 respectively, explicitly to bridge organizations that find migrating to GlassFish difficult.
insights.speaker: Michael Redlich
insights.so_what: Illustrates a recurring enterprise-software business model: third-party vendors monetizing extended support for abandoned or end-of-life enterprise Java platforms rather than forcing customers into disruptive migrations.
insights.quote: We decided to bridge this gap and start offering our supported builds of Payara to allow a smooth transition to a Payara Server build supported by OmniFish, without having to migrate to GlassFish to be able to benefit from our support services.

themes.theme: New runtime and agent tooling entrants

insights.claim: Vidocq, a new zero-external-dependency implementation of the Jakarta EE 11 Core Profile and MicroProfile 7.1 specifications built in pure Java for JDK 25, launched validated against 5,650 official TCK tests, adding a new competitor to the Jakarta EE runtime space.
insights.speaker: Michael Redlich
insights.so_what: A new lightweight, dependency-free Jakarta EE runtime entrant could pressure incumbent enterprise Java application servers on footprint and simplicity.

insights.claim: Google ADK for Java 1.6.0 added a ClassPathSkillSource class for loading agent skills directly from the Java classpath and the ability to advance a SequentialAgent to a paused sub-agent so an existing agent sequence can resume onward.
insights.speaker: Michael Redlich
insights.so_what: Shows Google actively iterating on Java-native agent development tooling, relevant for enterprises building agentic systems on the JVM rather than Python.
